tweets.text:
this year many teams have their lowest draft capital in over 20 years:

lowest since at least 1999: Broncos, Bengals

lowest since 2003 (23 yrs): Packers, Falcons

lowest since 2014 (12 yrs): Colts

lowest since 2018 (8 yrs): Jaguars

lowest since 2020 (6 yrs): Bills

NFC North updates:

R to @AdamSchefter: More about Broderick Jones and the Steelers: https://www.espn.com/nfl/draft2026/story/_/id/48507444/2026-nfl-draft-notes-intel-buzz-adam-schefter-first-round-picks-teams-trades

Steelers left tackle Broderick Jones suffered a setback to the neck injury he sustained last season, and is scheduled to be examined this week to determine whether he will be ready for training camp and/or the start of the season, per league sources.

There is growing chatter in league circles that Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love is in play with Arizona at No. 3 overall. Ideally, the Cardinals would like to find a team willing to trade up so they can acquire more picks, and there have already been “exploratory” calls, as one source described it, regarding the No. 3 selection. But if the Cardinals hold on to their pick, some around the league believe they could pull an early draft surprise and select Love.

https://www.espn.com/nfl/draft2026/story/_/id/48507444/2026-nfl-draft-notes-intel-buzz-adam-schefter-first-round-picks-teams-trades

The Patriots remain at the clear forefront of trade talks for AJ Brown, another team could always emerge, and there's a lot of time between now and June 1. But as of today, multiple sources believe Brown and the Patriots are likely to become a tandem.

https://www.espn.com/nfl/draft2026/story/_/id/48507444/2026-nfl-draft-notes-intel-buzz-adam-schefter-first-round-picks-teams-trades

The Eagles remain open to trading AJ Brown, the Patriots remain highly interested in acquiring the star receiver, and conversations are expected to resume shortly on or before June 1, likely culminating in a deal, per league sources.

As one source said about the Patriots' pursuit of Brown, they'll “definitely be engaging then.”

A trade to New England is now considered likely.

A.J. Brown is likely to become a Patriot post June 1, per ESPN sources.

This and more in ESPN’s annual NFL Draft intel column:

https://www.espn.com/nfl/draft2026/story/_/id/48507444/2026-nfl-draft-notes-intel-buzz-adam-schefter-first-round-picks-teams-trades

RT by @AdamSchefter: NFL Network has obtained the police report on the misdemeanor charges against Zachariah Branch, who was arrested while standing on a public sidewalk for not moving far enough when a police officer asked him to move:

“A male, later identified as Zacharia Branch, continued to stand on the sidewalk without making an attempt to move. I continued to give Zacharia Branch verbal commands to move from blocking the sidewalk and advised that if he did not, he would receive a citation for blocking the sidewalk. Zacharia Branch smirked, then stepped backwards and to the right, then remained standing upon the public sidewalk, so as to obstruct, hinder, and impede free passage upon the sidewalk as well as impede free ingress/egress to or from the adjacent places of business. Due to those actions and Zacharia Branch’s failure to comply with multiple verbal lawful commands, he was placed under arrest for misdemeanor Obstruction of LEO and received a citation for Obstructing Public Sidewalks.”

Former Georgia wide receiver Zachariah Branch, who is widely expected to be a Day 2 NFL draft pick, was arrested early Sunday morning in Athens, Georgia, on two possible misdemeanor charges.

More at espn: https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/48532665/nfl-draft-prospect-zachariah-branch-arrested-georgia

It's the Jets so they'll probably mess it up somehow BUT this offense could be looking nicey for a young QB in 2027:

-2025 top-100 draft capital: No. 2, No. 16, No. 33, No. 44
-2026 top-100 draft capital: NYJ/DAL/IND Round 1 picks, NYJ Round 2/3 picks.
-Most projected effective cap space in the league in 2027
-O-line: Definitely need to fortify the interior, but Membou and Fashanu pretty solid building blocks at tackle
-RB: Breece Hall is still only 24! Long-term deal seems to be on the table if he wants to stay
-WR: Garrett Wilson + top-four dude at No. 16. I also still believe in AD Mitchell but that's a conversation to have with my therapist another day
-TE: Mason Taylor turns 22 in May. Reasonable to believe he turns into a solid starter

I should add at least one spicy *East* series.

The Lakers certainly provided plenty last night, too.

Pretty unbelievable turn of events for Orlando to go from where the Magic were last Wednesday to obliterate Charlotte and then go into Detroit and win tonight. Pretty much no one outside of Orlando saw that one coming, and we have at least one spicy series now!

After 3: Celtics 95, 76ers 71

An absolute demolition by the Celtics through three quarters, as Boston's dominated virtually every phase of the game.

Philadelphia will hope to find a little momentum here in the fourth, but a very impressive start to the postseason for Boston.

This has been an absolute demolition so far by the Celtics. Philly can't hit a shot, Boston is shooting 55 percent from the field, isn't turning the ball over and Jayson Tatum is toying with the game.

After 1: Celtics 33, Sixers 18

If Philadelphia is going to have a chance in this series, it's got to fill it up from 3.

In the first, the Sixers went 1-for-9. Boston, meanwhile, was 10-for-16 on 2s. 

Safe to say that if that combo continues this will be a very short series.

Excited to cover another Celtics-Sixers series. Should be another interesting one, with it still unclear when Joel Embiid will be able to return, if at all, from his appendectomy.

But a lot of star power and scoring on both sides either way.

NFL Network has obtained the police report on the misdemeanor charges against Zachariah Branch, who was arrested while standing on a public sidewalk for not moving far enough when a police officer asked him to move:

“A male, later identified as Zacharia Branch, continued to stand on the sidewalk without making an attempt to move. I continued to give Zacharia Branch verbal commands to move from blocking the sidewalk and advised that if he did not, he would receive a citation for blocking the sidewalk. Zacharia Branch smirked, then stepped backwards and to the right, then remained standing upon the public sidewalk, so as to obstruct, hinder, and impede free passage upon the sidewalk as well as impede free ingress/egress to or from the adjacent places of business. Due to those actions and Zacharia Branch’s failure to comply with multiple verbal lawful commands, he was placed under arrest for misdemeanor Obstruction of LEO and received a citation for Obstructing Public Sidewalks.”
